title: fraxinus ornus subsp. garganica
intro: Fraxinus ornus subsp. garganica, also known as the Turkish ash, is a subspecies of the European ash tree, specifically found in the Gargano Peninsula in Italy. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its rich content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which gives it anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus ornus subsp. garganica include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and treating digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health concerns. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used to harness its medicinal benefits.
